events.text: Patient is a 52-year-old Caucasian male with no significant past medical history presenting for evaluation of a changing mole on his upper back.
At age 52, patient was diagnosed with malignant melanoma (ICD-O morphology 8720/3) of the skin of the trunk (ICD-O topography C44.5), clinically staged as Stage IIIC (T3bN2aM0).

At age 52, excisional biopsy of the upper back lesion revealed malignant melanoma with Breslow thickness of 2.8 mm, Clark level IV, with ulceration present, mitotic rate 5/mm2, microsatellites absent, and peripheral and deep margins positive necessitating re-excision.
At age 52, wide local excision with 2 cm margins of the primary melanoma site on the upper back was performed with primary closure.
At age 52, sentinel lymph node biopsy of the right axilla was performed, with 2 of 4 sentinel nodes positive for metastatic melanoma deposits measuring up to 3.2 mm.

At age 52, sentinel lymph node pathology showed metastatic melanoma in 2 of 4 nodes with extracapsular extension absent, largest deposit 3.2 mm, confirmed by S100, HMB-45, and Melan-A immunohistochemistry.
At age 52, completion right axillary lymph node dissection was performed, yielding 18 lymph nodes with no additional nodes positive for metastatic disease (0/14 additional nodes).

At age 52, next-generation sequencing of the primary tumor revealed BRAF V600E mutation (allele frequency 42%), NRAS wild-type, TERT promoter mutation (C228T), CDKN2A loss, and tumor mutational burden of 14 mutations per megabase.
At age 52, baseline PET/CT showed no evidence of distant metastatic disease, with post-surgical changes in the right axilla and upper back.
At age 52, MRI of the brain with and without gadolinium contrast showed no intracranial metastatic disease.
At age 52, LDH level was within normal limits at 185 U/L and the patient was deemed a candidate for adjuvant systemic therapy.
At age 52, adjuvant nivolumab 480 mg IV every 4 weeks was initiated for resected Stage IIIC melanoma per CheckMate 238 protocol.
At age 53, patient developed grade 2 immune-related diarrhea with 5-6 loose stools daily, managed with loperamide and close monitoring without treatment interruption.
At age 53, surveillance CT of chest, abdomen, and pelvis at 6 months showed no evidence of recurrent or metastatic disease.
At age 53, surveillance MRI brain at 6 months showed no intracranial metastatic disease.
At age 53, patient completed 12 months of adjuvant nivolumab therapy with no evidence of disease recurrence and LDH remained normal at 172 U/L.
At age 53, end-of-treatment PET/CT demonstrated complete metabolic response with no FDG-avid lesions suspicious for recurrent or metastatic melanoma.
At age 54, routine surveillance CT chest/abdomen/pelvis at 18 months post-surgery showed new suspicious 1.2 cm pulmonary nodule in the right lower lobe.
At age 54, PET/CT confirmed FDG-avid right lower lobe pulmonary nodule (SUVmax 6.8) and a new 1.5 cm FDG-avid subcutaneous nodule in the left flank, concerning for metastatic melanoma recurrence.
At age 54, CT-guided core biopsy of the right lower lobe pulmonary nodule confirmed metastatic melanoma, positive for S100 and SOX10, with BRAF V600E mutation confirmed on repeat molecular testing.
At age 54, patient was restaged as Stage IV melanoma (M1b) with pulmonary and subcutaneous metastases.
At age 54, serum LDH was elevated at 312 U/L consistent with progressive metastatic disease.
At age 54, combination dabrafenib 150 mg twice daily plus trametinib 2 mg daily was initiated for BRAF V600E-mutant Stage IV melanoma given rapid disease progression.

At age 54, patient developed grade 2 pyrexia (temperature 39.1 C) attributed to dabrafenib, managed with dose interruption and acetaminophen, with rechallenge at reduced dabrafenib dose of 100 mg twice daily.
At age 54, restaging CT at 8 weeks showed partial response with 40% reduction in pulmonary nodule and resolution of subcutaneous nodule per RECIST 1.1 criteria.
At age 55, after 10 months on dabrafenib/trametinib the patient demonstrated durable partial response with LDH normalized to 198 U/L and remained on continued BRAF/MEK inhibitor therapy.

scenario_blurb: Stage III/IV melanoma with BRAF V600E mutation. Treated with combination BRAF/MEK inhibitors (dabrafenib/trametinib) and/or checkpoint immunotherapy (nivolumab plus ipilimumab, pembrolizumab). Some with brain metastases. Include LDH levels.
